questions.question: Social psychology is the study of:
options: How individuals think, feel, and behave in social contexts

questions.question: Social influence refers to:
options: The effect of others on an individual's behavior and beliefs

questions.question: Conformity is defined as:
options: Changing behavior to match the group
Changing behavior to stand out from the group

questions.question: In Asch's experiment, participants conformed due to:
options: Normative social influence and informational social influence

questions.question: Normative social influence occurs when:
options: We conform to be accepted and avoid rejection

questions.question: Informational social influence occurs when:
options: We conform because we believe others have more information

questions.question: In Milgram's obedience experiment, participants:
options: Delivered what they believed were electric shocks to a learner

questions.question: The Stanford prison experiment was conducted by:
The Stanford prison experiment demonstrated:
options: The power of social roles and situations on behavior

questions.question: Attribution theory explains:
options: How we explain the causes of behavior

questions.question: Dispositional attribution explains behavior as due to:
Situational attribution explains behavior as due to:
The fundamental attribution error is:
options: Overestimating dispositional factors and underestimating situational factors

questions.question: The actor-observer bias is:
options: Attributing our own behavior to situations and others to dispositions
Attributing our own behavior to dispositions and others to situations

questions.question: Self-serving bias is:
options: Attributing success to internal factors and failure to external factors

questions.question: Cognitive dissonance theory was proposed by:
Cognitive dissonance occurs when:
options: Beliefs and behaviors are inconsistent, causing discomfort

questions.question: The foot-in-the-door phenomenon is:
options: Agreeing to a large request first
Agreeing to a small request then being more likely to agree to a large request

questions.question: The door-in-the-face technique is:
options: Making a large request first that is refused, then making a smaller request

questions.question: Prejudice is:
options: A positive attitude towards a group
A negative attitude towards a group based on stereotypes
A neutral attitude towards a group

questions.question: Discrimination is:
options: A behavior that treats people unfairly based on group membership

questions.question: Stereotypes are:
options: Generalized beliefs about a group that may be accurate or inaccurate

questions.question: Outgroup homogeneity effect is:
options: Perceiving outgroup members as more similar to each other than they are

questions.question: The contact hypothesis suggests:
options: Prejudice decreases with contact under appropriate conditions
Contact has no effect on prejudice

questions.question: Social identity theory was proposed by:
Scapegoat theory explains prejudice as:
options: Blaming a group for problems that are not their fault

questions.question: Realistic conflict theory suggests prejudice arises from:
Altruism is defined as:
options: Helping others without expecting anything in return

questions.question: The bystander effect is:
options: Increased helping when others are present
Decreased helping when others are present

questions.question: Diffusion of responsibility refers to:
options: Reduced personal responsibility when others are present

questions.question: Pluralistic ignorance is:
options: Misinterpreting a situation because others are not reacting

questions.question: Kitty Genovese case is often cited to illustrate:
Aggression is defined as:
options: Any behavior intended to help others
Any behavior intended to harm another

questions.question: Hostile aggression is:
options: Aggression driven by anger with the intent to harm

questions.question: Instrumental aggression is:
options: Aggression as a means to achieve a goal

questions.question: Catharsis is the idea that:
options: Aggression is reduced by performing an aggressive act
Aggression is increased by performing an aggressive act

questions.question: Groupthink is:
options: The desire for harmony that overrides critical thinking

questions.question: Deindividuation is:
options: Loss of self-awareness and individuality in groups

questions.question: Mere exposure effect means:
options: We dislike things we are exposed to
We like things more when we are repeatedly exposed to them
Exposure has no effect on liking
We like things less when exposed to them

questions.question: Physical attractiveness is often associated with:
The halo effect is:
options: Judging a person negatively based on one trait
Judging a person positively based on one trait

questions.question: The just-world hypothesis is:
options: Belief that the world is unfair
Belief that the world is fair and people get what they deserve
Belief that the world is random
Belief that the world is unpredictable

questions.question: Self-fulfilling prophecy occurs when:
options: Beliefs about others have no effect
Beliefs about others cause them to behave in ways that confirm the beliefs
Beliefs about others are always wrong
Beliefs about others are always accurate
